android_specific_rule.reason: Android platform compatibility property affected by the distributed binaries and the SDK linker policy supplied by this distribution

decision_basis: Astral-thin responsibility follows the distributed Python artifact and Android-specific ABI/runtime requirements; repair of later user-created wheels belongs to the user or external packaging tooling
diagnostic_only_for_user_built_wheels: wheel filename, hash, size, and extension identity
distributor_hard_gates: deterministic distributed artifact bytes and receipts
distributed archive structure, manifest, checksums, relocation, runtime, subprocess, venv, and claimed uv consumer paths
distributed native ELF dependency closure and absence of accidental host or Termux identity in distributed artifact bytes
truthful Android target identity and API floor
profile M native extension build, installation, and import capability
16 KiB LOAD alignment for every distributed ELF object
16 KiB LOAD alignment for the baseline extension produced by the SDK compile and link flags supplied by profile M
explicitly_out_of_scope: automatic removal of RPATH or RUNPATH from user-built wheels
repackaging or publication portability of user-created wheels
reproducibility of arbitrary user package builds

project_action.historical_explicit_normalization_eviden: retained as historical experiment evidence but removed from current RB-3 acceptance and successor audit gates
project_action.possible_future_work: separate toy project or external Android wheel repair tool
